Edward ScissorhandsÂ is a 1990 AmericanÂ gothicÂ romantic fantasyÂ film[5]Â directed byÂ Tim Burton. It was produced by Burton andÂ Denise Di Novi, written byÂ Caroline ThompsonÂ from a story by her and Burton, and starringÂ Johnny DeppÂ as the title character, along withÂ Winona Ryder,Â Dianne Wiest,Â Anthony Michael Hall,Â Kathy Baker,Â Vincent Price, andÂ Alan Arkin. It tells the story of an unfinished artificial humanoid who has scissor blades instead of hands, is taken in by aÂ suburbanÂ family, and falls in love with their teenage daughter.

Burton conceivedÂ Edward ScissorhandsÂ from his childhood upbringing in suburbanÂ Burbank, California. DuringÂ pre-productionÂ ofÂ Beetlejuice, Thompson was hired to adapt Burton's story into a screenplay, and the film began development atÂ 20th Century FoxÂ afterÂ Warner Bros.Â declined.Â Edward ScissorhandsÂ was thenÂ fast-trackedÂ after Burton's critical and financial success withÂ Batman. The film also marks the fourth collaboration between Burton andÂ film scoreÂ composerÂ Danny Elfman, and was Vincent Price's last film role to be released in his lifetime.

Edward ScissorhandsÂ was a critical and commercial success, grossing over four times its $20Â million budget. The film won theÂ British Academy Film Award for Best Production DesignÂ and theÂ Hugo Award for Best Dramatic Presentation, in addition to receiving multiple nominations at theÂ Academy Awards,Â British Academy Film Awards, and theÂ Saturn Awards. Both Burton and Elfman considerÂ Edward ScissorhandsÂ their most personal and favorite work.
